quotes.text: Kenneth Arnold, June 24 1947: nine objects "like saucers skipping across water" near Mount Rainier. The phrase was coined that afternoon.
Betty and Barney Hill described their 1961 abduction under hypnosis; Betty's hand-drawn star map matched Zeta Reticuli well enough to trouble astronomers.
The 1995 "Alien Autopsy" film, hyped on Fox prime-time, was admitted by Ray Santilli twenty years later to be a reconstruction.
The Majestic-12 documents arrived anonymously by mail in 1984. The FBI eventually filed them under "completely bogus."
Albert Bender's 1953 visitors are the canonical Men in Black — three pale figures who appeared in his bedroom and told him to stop investigating UFOs.
In 2017 the Pentagon admitted that the AATIP program had studied unexplained military encounters, and quietly released the three Navy 'tic-tac' videos.
70,000 people in Fátima, Portugal, October 13, 1917, reported the sun dancing in the sky — sometimes cited as the first modern mass UAP sighting.
Hessdalen Valley in Norway has had recurring unexplained light phenomena since the 1980s — studied by scientists, never explained.
